company_fit.why: Baseten builds ML model serving infrastructure (Truss) used by real production teams. Cold start and GPU utilization are active engineering problems. The codebase is open source and the team is reachable via HN and GitHub.
company_fit.sponsorship_notes: Check current JD for H1B sponsorship language

open_problem.title: Cold start latency in GPU-backed model serving
open_problem.description: When a model container goes idle, the next request pays full container startup + weight loading cost. For 7B+ models this exceeds 30 seconds. No open benchmark exists that breaks down latency by stage across serving frameworks.
open_problem.contribution_surface: Reproducible cold start benchmark; lightweight traffic-based pre-loading prototype

people_map.name: Phil Howes
people_map.relevance_reason: Co-founder; may not be the right cold start contact but can route to the right engineer
people_map.outreach_note: Baseten's public architecture writeup on Truss model packaging

adjacent_proof: Luis is a CS student at UConn with coursework in Operating Systems and ML inference (CSE 4705, CSE 4820). He is working through cold start timing in open-source serving frameworks as a self-directed project and has read through the Truss codebase and vLLM architecture. He has not deployed production-scale serving but can prototype and benchmark at the 7B model level on consumer hardware.
